problem.title: 4Sum
problem.description: Given an array nums of n integers, return an array of all the unique quadruplets [nums[a], nums[b], nums[c], nums[d]] such that: 0 <= a, b, c, d < n, a, b, c, and d are distinct, nums[a] + nums[b] + nums[c] + nums[d] == target

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Two Pointers
solutions.description: Sort the array and use two nested loops to fix the first two elements, then apply the two-pointer technique on the remaining subarray to find pairs that bring the sum to the target. Duplicate values are skipped at every level to avoid repeated quadruplets. This generalises the 3Sum pattern by adding one more outer loop at O(n³) total.
